headline: Tubi Doubles Down in Canada, Taps Digital Veteran Gave Lindo as First Market GM
lede: Fox-owned free streamer Tubi is making a strategic play in the Great White North with the appointment of digital media veteran Gave Lindo as its inaugural Canadian general manager.
body:
Tubi is signaling serious expansion intentions in the Canadian streaming landscape by hiring Gave Lindo, a seasoned digital media executive with deep roots in both public broadcasting and social media platforms.

Lindo arrives with an impressive digital pedigree, having previously served as Executive Director of OTT Programming at the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation (CBC) and holding significant leadership roles at TikTok. His appointment underscores Tubi's commitment to localizing content strategy and understanding the nuanced Canadian media market.

The move comes as part of Tubi's broader international growth strategy, with the newly established Toronto office serving as a critical hub for regional content development and market penetration. Lindo's expertise in digital programming and platform management positions him uniquely to navigate the competitive streaming ecosystem.

Industry watchers note that Tubi's expansion represents a calculated bet on the growing appetite for free, ad-supported streaming services in Canada. The market has shown increasing receptivity to alternative viewing models beyond traditional subscription platforms.

With Lindo at the helm, Tubi aims to leverage his deep understanding of digital media trends and local content preferences to carve out a distinctive market position.
key_facts: First dedicated Canadian GM for Tubi
Part of Tubi's international expansion strategy
